summary.intent: Implement the framework-agnostic single-thumb Slider core component (#244) for the ars-ui component library, including discrete/value-label API, ARIA attributes, pointer geometry helpers, value math, and snapshot coverage.
summary.outcome: The slider agnostic core was fully implemented, audited, and landed in a non-draft PR with `Closes #244`. All verification gates passed: 26 unit tests, spec conformance, proptest invariants, 23 insta snapshots, clippy, fmt, spec validation, corpus compile, and a complete mutation run with 0 missed mutants out of 233 tested.

repo: The 'no DOM geometry in core' rule is enforced: adapters must supply logical coordinates via `SliderPointer + Rect`; `PlatformEffects::get_bounding_rect` must not be called from agnostic core.

New agnostic components follow the `switch`/`number_input` machine pattern: `ars_core::Machine` with `type Effect`, `on_props_changed` for controlled value sync, and `Bindable` for controlled/uncontrolled duality.
`#[derive(ComponentPart)]` requires `Eq`/`Hash`; enum variants carrying `f64` (like `Marker { value: f64 }`) require manual impls using `value.to_bits()` for bitwise float identity.
The `--unreferenced=reject` snapshot audit must be run with `--features i18n` to avoid false failures from feature-gated highlight snapshot tests.
Proptest slow/nightly tests use an `--ignored` convention; they compile but are skipped in the default test run and explicitly invoked with `-- --ignored`.
PRs that introduce `.snap` files require the `snapshot-reviewed` label.
`cargo xci-fast` is the mandatory final pre-push gate; it runs 6000+ tests across workspace unit slices and integration targets.
GitHub Project board item status is updated via `gh project item-edit` with field and option IDs fetched from the project field-list API.
`cargo mutants` is a required verification step, not optional; survivors must be addressed with new tests rather than exclusions unless genuinely equivalent.

The spec (`spec/components/input/slider.md`) contained a DOM-shaped `compute_value_from_pointer(event: &PointerEvent, ...)` example that conflicted with the agnostic architecture — spec correction is in-scope for the same PR.

code.finding: Part::Marker { value: f64 } cannot use #[derive(ComponentPart)] because f64 does not implement Eq/Hash; manual impls using value.to_bits() are required for marker identity.

Bindable<T> is the controlled/uncontrolled value abstraction; is_controlled() and value sync must both be handled in on_props_changed to avoid stale internal values when leaving controlled mode with changed bounds.
snap_to_step must guard against non-positive and non-finite step values by returning the clamped value directly; mutation testing surfaced this as an uncovered edge.
value_from_pointer uses (max - min) as the range denominator; tests with min=0 make max-min and max+min indistinguishable, so non-zero min cases are required to catch sign-flip mutants.
on_value_change must not fire when the new snapped value equals the current value; a mutation survivor exposed that the no-op guard was untested.
highlight proptest and spec_conformance tests require a cfg guard so they are not compiled when the i18n feature is absent, otherwise the full `--unreferenced=reject` snapshot audit fails.

workflow: Mutation testing with cargo-mutants should be run interactively with periodic result-file polling (`wc -l mutants.out/caught.txt missed.txt`) rather than waiting for terminal output, which can be sparse.
Kill-and-patch cycles on cargo-mutants are effective: stop on first survivor, fix the real gap, restart — rather than waiting for all survivors before patching.
TDD red-green cycle: write failing tests that reference the planned API surface first, confirm they fail for the expected reason (missing impl), then implement.
Post-implementation audit as a formal checklist step (invoking a SKILL.md) caught real drift (controlled bounds sync, Eq contract on Part) before the code left the worktree.
Moving the GitHub issue to In Progress before any code changes is a required step in this repo's workflow.
Running `cargo +nightly fmt --all` after mutation-driven test additions is necessary because the formatter has opinions on assertion layout and import grouping that clippy then enforces.

friction: cargo-mutants required 6+ restarts across the session; each restart rebuilds all 233 mutants from scratch (~32s each), making a full clean pass take 30-60 minutes.
`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` without `--features i18n` incorrectly fails due to feature-gated highlight fixtures — this is a non-obvious footgun for new contributors.
The slider spec contained a DOM-shaped geometry API example that directly contradicted the issue's agnostic architecture requirement, requiring spec surgery as unplanned work.
Cargo job serialization on the shared build lock caused test commands to queue behind each other rather than running in parallel, adding wall-clock time.

Mutation survivors in float comparison helpers (props_output_changed, value_percent, value_from_pointer) required multiple targeted test additions to fully close because sign-opposite and epsilon cases are easy to miss in initial coverage.
open_items: Leptos adapter component for Slider not yet implemented — explicitly out of scope for #244.
Dioxus adapter component for Slider not yet implemented — explicitly out of scope for #244.
RangeSlider / N-thumb implementation (spec §6) deferred to a future issue.
Browser/E2E tests for Slider interaction excluded from this task.
Adapter-layer slider specs (spec/leptos-components/input/slider.md, spec/dioxus-components/input/slider.md) will need updating when adapters are built.
